question_text:
"Some football fans admire various clubs, others love only a single team. But who is a fan of whom precisely? The following argument pertains to this question: First premise: Mario is a friend of FK Žalgiris Vilnius. Second premise: Being a follower of F.C. Copenhagen is necessary for being a friend of FK Žalgiris Vilnius. It follows that Mario is a follower of F.C. Copenhagen."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: First premise: Being a friend of SC Braga is sufficient for being a backer of Tottenham Hotspur. From this follows: Every backer of Tottenham Hotspur is a friend of SC Braga."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: First premise: Every classmate of Peggy is a classmate of Alexandra. Second premise: Some classmate of Alexandra is not a schoolmate of Cindi. We may conclude that not every schoolmate of Cindi is a classmate of Peggy."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Titanium oxide an ingredient of my washing power? Which chemicals does my perfume contain? It is really difficult to keep track of all chemicals one is regularly exposed to. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First of all, no ingredient of Laquer Ink that is an ingredient of Cheryl PEARLBLANC is an ingredient of PANDA LIP BALM. All this entails that not being an ingredient of PANDA LIP BALM is sufficient for being an ingredient of Cheryl PEARLBLANC and an ingredient of Laquer Ink."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: First premise: Being a regular consumer of Sunsilk shampoo is sufficient for not being an owner of a Bed Head shampoo. Second premise: Every regular consumer of Sunsilk shampoo is not a loyal buyer of Aveda shampoo or not an owner of a Bed Head shampoo. Therefore, no regular consumer of Sunsilk shampoo is a loyal buyer of Aveda shampoo."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Fred a fan of Liverpool? Are supporters of Real Madrid devotees of PSG? In European football, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of the mutual admiration and dislike. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First premise: Somebody is a fan of Fenerbahçe SK and a devotee of AS Monaco FC. Second premise: Being a member of Getafe CF is necessary for being a devotee of AS Monaco FC. In consequence, there is somebody who is a fan of Fenerbahçe SK and a member of Getafe CF."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Fred a cousin of Robert? Is Joe related to Bob? In large families, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of all one's relatives. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: Whoever is a nephew of Adam or a grandson of Walter is a brother of Arturo. Charles is a nephew of Adam. Hence, Charles is a brother of Arturo."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Siri a stepsister of Mary? Is Susan related to Kate? In large families, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of all one's relatives. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: To begin with, everyone who is a close friend of Marilyn is a sister of Nelda, too. Moreover, being a classmate of Elaine is sufficient for being a sister of Nelda. Finally, every workmate of Bianca is a close friend of Marilyn or a classmate of Elaine. We may conclude: Everyone who is a workmate of Bianca is a sister of Nelda, too."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

In the planning of a new district in a township, it was decided to build a special community in the southeast, northwest, centered on the citizen park. These four communities are designated as cultural area, leisure area, commercial area and administrative service area. It is known that the administrative service area is southwest of the cultural area, and the cultural area is southeast of the leisure area.

Based on the above statement, which of the following can be derived?

A. Civic Park is north of the administrative service area.
B. The leisure area is southwest of the cultural area.
C. The cultural district is in the northeast of the business district.
D. The business district is southeast of the leisure area.

options: Civic Park is north of the administrative service area.
The leisure area is southwest of the cultural area.
The cultural district is in the northeast of the business district.
The business district is southeast of the leisure area.

question_text:
In rheumatoid arthritis, the body' s immune system misfunctions by attacking healthy cells in the joints causing the release of a hormone that in turn causes pain and swelling. This hormone is normally activated only in reaction to injury or infection. A new arthritis medication will contain a protein that inhibits the functioning of the hormone that causes pain and swelling in the joints.

The statements above, if true, most strongly support which one of the following conclusions?

A. Unlike aspirin and other medications that reduce pain and swelling and that are currently available, the new medication would repair existing cell damage that had been caused by rheumatoid arthritis.
B. A patient treated with the new medication for rheumatoid arthritis could sustain a joint injury without becoming aware of it.
C. Joint diseases other than rheumatoid arthritis would not be affected by the new medication.
D. The benefits to rheumatoid arthritis sufferers of the new medication would outweigh the medication's possible harmful side effects.

options: Unlike aspirin and other medications that reduce pain and swelling and that are currently available, the new medication would repair existing cell damage that had been caused by rheumatoid arthritis.
A patient treated with the new medication for rheumatoid arthritis could sustain a joint injury without becoming aware of it.
Joint diseases other than rheumatoid arthritis would not be affected by the new medication.
The benefits to rheumatoid arthritis sufferers of the new medication would outweigh the medication's possible harmful side effects.

question_text:
After graduating from high school, people rarely do math problems or talk about history, but people face daily decisions related to the family economy. However, when mathematics and history are compulsory in the high school curriculum, family economics is only an elective Class, and very few students take this course.

Which of the following points () are best supported by the above discussion.

A. Unless high school focuses more on non-professional subjects like family economics, people who graduate from high school will never easily make decisions in daily life.
B. Although family economics is the most useful discipline for people who have to make decisions in their daily lives, usually mathematics and history can also help people make these decisions.
C. Mathematics, history and other subjects that are not directly related to one's daily life should not be a required part of the high school curriculum.
D. If it is important to teach high school students about disciplines that they will face later in everyday life, then home economics should be an important part of the high school curriculum.

options: Unless high school focuses more on non-professional subjects like family economics, people who graduate from high school will never easily make decisions in daily life.
Although family economics is the most useful discipline for people who have to make decisions in their daily lives, usually mathematics and history can also help people make these decisions.
Mathematics, history and other subjects that are not directly related to one's daily life should not be a required part of the high school curriculum.
If it is important to teach high school students about disciplines that they will face later in everyday life, then home economics should be an important part of the high school curriculum.

question_text:
Shadowless lamp effect: refers to a holistic mechanism formed by the interaction of many factors, which can reduce the incompleteness or shortcomings of a problem to a minimum, or even disappear, and finally make this problem well resolved.

Which of the following are not the shadowless effect?

A. In 2012, the number of Chinese Internet users exceeded 500 million. More and more people used Internet media to fully obtain and disseminate information. Information and public opinion have also been more widely distributed in three dimensions. China is forming a transparent information space and public opinion.
B. In order to form an effective mechanism for a vigorous green ecological civilization and fully concentrate the wisdom of the people, a city has set up a civic supervision committee, a civic hearing, and an environmental protection organization, so that the general public can better advise and play a supervisory role.
C. In order to prevent doctors from taking medical rebates, a province has set up a special patient complaint hotline for patients in various hospitals.
D. In order to manage the private use of public buses, a local government takes photos of the suspected cars at any time and sends them to.

options: In 2012, the number of Chinese Internet users exceeded 500 million. More and more people used Internet media to fully obtain and disseminate information. Information and public opinion have also been more widely distributed in three dimensions. China is forming a transparent information space and public opinion.

In order to form an effective mechanism for a vigorous green ecological civilization and fully concentrate the wisdom of the people, a city has set up a civic supervision committee, a civic hearing, and an environmental protection organization, so that the general public can better advise and play a supervisory role.
In order to prevent doctors from taking medical rebates, a province has set up a special patient complaint hotline for patients in various hospitals.
In order to manage the private use of public buses, a local government takes photos of the suspected cars at any time and sends them to.

question_text:
Absolute antonyms: also known as complementary antonyms, these antonyms are mutually exclusive in the sense that positive A must negate B, positive B must negate A; meanwhile, negative A must affirm B, and negative B must affirm A.

According to the definition above, which of the following is the following set of absolute antonyms?

A. Complete-Incomplete.
B. Happy-angry.
C. Cold-warm.
D. Humble-noble.

options: Complete-Incomplete.
Happy-angry.
Cold-warm.
Humble-noble.

question_text:
Brick and Mortar Bookstore Owner: Bookstores are the backbone of our country. Democracies depend on a literate population, and reading fosters the creativity necessary to drive innovation. Brick and mortar bookstores introduce people to new books and entice people to expand their literary preferences. Without brick and mortar bookstores, the demand for books would collapse, killing the publishing industry. Digital Bookstore Owner: There is no denying the importance of reading for any democracy. However, digital books are the future. People can easily access our enormous catalogue of books, which is far greater than any brick and mortar bookstore. We provide synopses and reviews that allow people to discover new interests. In addition, digital books are cheaper than paper books.

What is the main point of dispute in the two arguments?

A. Customers prefer paper books.
B. Digital bookstores depend on the existence of brick and mortar bookstores.
C. Digital books are cheaper than paper books.
D. Digital books will someday replace paper books altogether.

options: Customers prefer paper books.
Digital bookstores depend on the existence of brick and mortar bookstores.
Digital books are cheaper than paper books.
Digital books will someday replace paper books altogether.

question_text:
All police officers carry guns, and all soldiers carry guns; ttherefore, police officers and soldiers are similar.

Which one of the following most closely parallels the argument?

A. All dogs have fur, and all mammals have fur; ttherefore, all dogs are mammals.
B. Farms and fisheries are similar. All farms produce food, and all fisheries produce food.
C. Some professional football players lift weights every day, and all bodybuilders lift weights every day; ttherefore, football players and bodybuilders are similar.
D. All apples have seeds and all grapefruits have seeds.

options: All dogs have fur, and all mammals have fur; ttherefore, all dogs are mammals.
Farms and fisheries are similar. All farms produce food, and all fisheries produce food.
Some professional football players lift weights every day, and all bodybuilders lift weights every day; ttherefore, football players and bodybuilders are similar.
All apples have seeds and all grapefruits have seeds.

question_text:
Heart disease is closely related to fatty embolism in arterial blood vessels, but a new study suggests that fat molecules may come not only from food but also from a certain type of bacteria in the mouth. Researchers believe that oral diseases are often related to heart disease.

Which of the following, if true, would best support the researcher's point of view?

A. Heart disease is usually caused by atherosclerosis, during which arterial blood vessels are blocked by a substance called "lipids".
B. The lipid molecules produced by this bacterium are different from the composition of lipids in mammals and belong to a special bacterial family.
C. In general, these bacteria only remain active in the mouth, and they may cause periodontal disease in the right environment.
D. Cytokines caused by oral diseases can destroy islet glands, reduce the amount of necessary insulin secreted by the body, and easily cause diabetes.

options: Heart disease is usually caused by atherosclerosis, during which arterial blood vessels are blocked by a substance called "lipids".
The lipid molecules produced by this bacterium are different from the composition of lipids in mammals and belong to a special bacterial family.
In general, these bacteria only remain active in the mouth, and they may cause periodontal disease in the right environment.
Cytokines caused by oral diseases can destroy islet glands, reduce the amount of necessary insulin secreted by the body, and easily cause diabetes.

question_text:
Customer: Boss, why is there no fish in the shredded pork? Boss: What's weird about this! Is there a wife in his wife's cake?

Which of the following dialogues is the most similar or identical to the above?

A. Teacher: Xiaoming, why didn't you do the homework I assigned yesterday? Xiaoming: Sorry, teacher! Is it necessary to do homework without going home?
B. Wife: Dear, why is there no money in your wallet? Husband: This is nothing abnormal! Is there always tea in the teapot?
C. Child: Mom, why is there no cotton in marshmallows? Mother: This is normal! Did you hide a hero in the hero brand ink?
D. Customer: Little sister, why are there no pearls in the pearl milk tea you sell? Salesperson: What a curious thing! There are no couples in the couple's lungs!

options: Teacher: Xiaoming, why didn't you do the homework I assigned yesterday? Xiaoming: Sorry, teacher! Is it necessary to do homework without going home?
Wife: Dear, why is there no money in your wallet? Husband: This is nothing abnormal! Is there always tea in the teapot?
Child: Mom, why is there no cotton in marshmallows? Mother: This is normal! Did you hide a hero in the hero brand ink?
Customer: Little sister, why are there no pearls in the pearl milk tea you sell? Salesperson: What a curious thing! There are no couples in the couple's lungs!

question_text:
"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: To start with, there is somebody who is a regular user of Zest soap and an owner of a The Body Shop soap. Now, nobody is neither an owner of a The Body Shop soap nor an infrequent user of Pre De Provence soap. So, necessarily, some infrequent user of Pre De Provence soap is not a regular user of Zest soap."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Siri a stepsister of Mary? Is Susan related to Kate? In large families, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of all one's relatives. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First of all, no aunt of Daisy is both a half-sister of Margie and an aunt of Dori. Next, whoever is not a half-sister of Margie is a sister of Susan. Plus,being a sister of Susan is necessary for not being an aunt of Dori. We may conclude: Being an aunt of Daisy is sufficient for being a sister of Susan."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

Natural landscape mainly refers to the naturally formed terrain, landforms and features such as plains, mountains, grasslands, forests, the sea, swamps and other scenery. These scenery are just unit ecosystems, so they are also called ecological landscapes.

Can it be derived from this text?

A. Natural landscape is more important than human landscape.
B. Natural landscape is more beautiful than human landscape.
C. Cities and towns are not natural.
D. Cities and towns are also natural landscapes.

options: Natural landscape is more important than human landscape.
Natural landscape is more beautiful than human landscape.
Cities and towns are not natural.
Cities and towns are also natural landscapes.

question_text:
"Is Fred a fan of Liverpool? Are supporters of Real Madrid devotees of PSG? In European football, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of the mutual admiration and dislike. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First, Teresa is a backer of FC Dinamo Minsk. Second, being a devotee of FK Sūduva is necessary for being a backer of FC Dinamo Minsk. We may conclude: Teresa is a devotee of FK Sūduva."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Fred a cousin of Robert? Is Joe related to Bob? In large families, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of all one's relatives. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: Roland is a classmate of Chris. Being a classmate of Chris is sufficient for not being a classmate of Forrest. It follows that Roland is not a classmate of Forrest."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: If someone is not a fan of FK Crvena zvezda, then that person is not a supporter of Paris Saint-Germain or not a supporter of AS Saint-Étienne. Nobody is neither an expert of Burnley FC nor a supporter of Paris Saint-Germain. Being a supporter of AS Saint-Étienne is necessary for not being an expert of Burnley FC. From this follows: Nobody is neither an expert of Burnley FC nor a fan of FK Crvena zvezda."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: Whoever is a supporter of FC Augsburg is not a devotee of Royal Antwerp FC. Patrick is not a supporter of FC Augsburg. Hence, it is not the case that Patrick is a devotee of Royal Antwerp FC."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

Information Poverty Alleviation: refers to the use of information technology to solve the economic and cultural poverty problems caused by information occlusion.

The following are information for poverty alleviation.

A. A logistics company teamed up with an Internet giant to recruit a group of courier from poor households in remote mountainous areas. After professional training, they took up posts to help them embark on the path of getting rid of poverty and getting rich.
B. A mobile phone manufacturer for the rural market introduced a low-cost, easy-to-operate, smart phone with a large screen and a long standby time. Villagers have replaced their old mobile phones.
C. A key middle school in the provincial capital has set up an online classroom and remotely synchronized live broadcast to a middle school in a poor county, so that teachers and students in both places share high-quality teaching resources. In recent years, the number of students admitted to key universities in the county has increased significantly.
D. Xiao Zhou, who returned home after graduation, took a few classmates to open an online store and sold his own planted red dates online. Sales gradually increased and the days became more and more prosperous.

options: A logistics company teamed up with an Internet giant to recruit a group of courier from poor households in remote mountainous areas. After professional training, they took up posts to help them embark on the path of getting rid of poverty and getting rich.
A mobile phone manufacturer for the rural market introduced a low-cost, easy-to-operate, smart phone with a large screen and a long standby time. Villagers have replaced their old mobile phones.

A key middle school in the provincial capital has set up an online classroom and remotely synchronized live broadcast to a middle school in a poor county, so that teachers and students in both places share high-quality teaching resources. In recent years, the number of students admitted to key universities in the county has increased significantly.

Xiao Zhou, who returned home after graduation, took a few classmates to open an online store and sold his own planted red dates online. Sales gradually increased and the days became more and more prosperous.

question_text:
"Some football fans admire various clubs, others love only a single team. But who is a fan of whom precisely? The following argument pertains to this question: Everyone who is both an admirer of Southampton FC and a supporter of Manchester City FC is not a critic of Arsenal FC. There is somebody who is an admirer of SL Benfica and a critic of Arsenal FC. It follows that there is somebody who is an admirer of SL Benfica and not both an admirer of Southampton FC and a supporter of Manchester City FC."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

According to a review of 61 studies of patients suffering from severely debilitating depression, a large majority of the patients reported that missing a night' s sleep immediately lifted their depression. Yet sleep-deprivation is not used to treat depression even though the conventional treatments, which use drugs and electric shocks, often have serious side effects.

Which of the following, if true, best explains the fact that sleep-deprivation is not used as a treatment for depression?

A. The dramatic shifts in mood connected with sleep and wakefulness have not been traced to particular changes in brain chemistry.
B. Keeping depressed patients awake is more difficult than keeping awake people who are not depressed.
C. Depression returns in full force as soon as the patient sleeps for even a few minutes.
D. For a small percentage of depressed patients, missing a night's sleep induces a temporary sense of euphoria.

options: The dramatic shifts in mood connected with sleep and wakefulness have not been traced to particular changes in brain chemistry.
Keeping depressed patients awake is more difficult than keeping awake people who are not depressed.
Depression returns in full force as soon as the patient sleeps for even a few minutes.
For a small percentage of depressed patients, missing a night's sleep induces a temporary sense of euphoria.

question_text:
A football coach teaches his players like this: "Football matches are always based on results. In football matches, you are either a winner or a loser; in the eyes of the fans, you are either a brave or a weak person. Winners are brave in the eyes of fans, so every loser is weak in the eyes of fans. ".

In order for the above football coach's argument to be valid, which of the following must be assumed?

A. In the eyes of the fans, the brave on the pitch will win.
B. Fans have accurate judgment to distinguish between bravery and cowardice.
C. The brave in the eyes of fans, not necessarily the true brave.
D. Even on the field, winning or losing is not the only criterion that distinguishes bravery and cowardice.

options: In the eyes of the fans, the brave on the pitch will win.
Fans have accurate judgment to distinguish between bravery and cowardice.
The brave in the eyes of fans, not necessarily the true brave.
Even on the field, winning or losing is not the only criterion that distinguishes bravery and cowardice.

question_text:
A unit recruited 30 new employees this year.① Some of the newly recruited employees are from outside the country; ② Of the newly recruited employees, the highest academic qualifications are not foreigners; ③ Some of the newly recruited employees are not foreign.

If only one of the above three judgments is true, then the following items correctly represent the number of foreign workers among the newly recruited employees in the unit.

A. Only 1 person is a foreigner.
B. 0 employees are foreigners.
C. 0 employees are not foreigners.
D. Only 1 person is not a foreigner.

options: Only 1 person is a foreigner.
0 employees are foreigners.
0 employees are not foreigners.
Only 1 person is not a foreigner.

question_text:
Computer store manager: Last year we made an average of 13 percent profit on the high-end computer models -- those priced over $1, 000 -- that we sold, while low-end models -- those priced below $1, 000 -- typically returned at least 25 percent profit. Since there is a limit to how many models we can display and sell, we should sell only low-end models. This would maximize our profits, since we would probably sell as many low-end models if that is all we sold as we would sell both kinds combined if we continued to sell both.

The reasoning in the manager's argument is vulnerable to criticism on which one of the following grounds?

A. The argument ignores the possibility that some customers who come into a computer store expecting to purchase a low-end model end up purchasing a high-end model.
B. The argument fails to address the possibility that, despite the price differential, the store sold as many high-end models as low-end models last year.
C. The argument fails to recognize that future sales of low-end computers may not be the same as past sales.
D. The argument fails to consider the possibility that the money earned on each high-end computer is significantly higher than the money earned on each low-end computer.

options: The argument ignores the possibility that some customers who come into a computer store expecting to purchase a low-end model end up purchasing a high-end model.
The argument fails to address the possibility that, despite the price differential, the store sold as many high-end models as low-end models last year.
The argument fails to recognize that future sales of low-end computers may not be the same as past sales.
The argument fails to consider the possibility that the money earned on each high-end computer is significantly higher than the money earned on each low-end computer.

question_text:
In different countries and at different times, the science and technology management system can be different and innovative, but there is one system that is particularly important and universal: that is the generalization of the subject of innovation, or the nationalization or people's nature of the daily innovation strategy. Let everyone have the heart to seek change and innovation, so that every invention, regardless of its size, has the opportunity to be displayed. It should be the basic starting point for building a large-scale independent innovation system and environment.

What does this passage mean?

A. Importance of science and technology management systems.
B. Innovation requires a good environment for support.
C. Innovation requires institutional support.
D. The sci-tech management system should guarantee innovation for all people.

options: Importance of science and technology management systems.
Innovation requires a good environment for support.
Innovation requires institutional support.
The sci-tech management system should guarantee innovation for all people.

question_text:
"Is Fred a fan of Liverpool? Are supporters of Real Madrid devotees of PSG? In European football, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of the mutual admiration and dislike. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First, being an ex-supporter of Gabala SC is necessary for not being a critic of Sevilla FC. Second, being an ex-supporter of Gabala SC is necessary for not being a supporter of SCR Altach. Third, no devotee of GNK Dinamo Zagreb is both a critic of Sevilla FC and a supporter of SCR Altach. From this follows: Everyone who is a devotee of GNK Dinamo Zagreb is an ex-supporter of Gabala SC, too."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"It is not always easy to see who is related to whom -- and in which ways. The following argument pertains to this question: First, some great-grandmother of Georgia is an aunt of Carole. Second, no workmate of Brandy who is a cousin of Edna is an aunt of Carole. We may conclude that there is somebody who is a great-grandmother of Georgia and, in addition, not a workmate of Brandy or not a cousin of Edna."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Fred a fan of Liverpool? Are supporters of Real Madrid devotees of PSG? In European football, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of the mutual admiration and dislike. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: To begin with, whoever is an ex-fan of West Ham United FC and a friend of Gabala SC is not an ex-fan of Hibernian FC. Therefore, every ex-fan of Hibernian FC is not an ex-fan of West Ham United FC or not a friend of Gabala SC."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Is Fred a cousin of Robert? Is Joe related to Bob? In large families, it is sometimes difficult to keep track of all one's relatives. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First, everyone who is neither a close friend of Bobby nor a classmate of Nicholas is a great-grandfather of Sean or a nephew of Will. Second, every great-grandfather of Sean is a workmate of Louis. Third, being a workmate of Louis is necessary for being a nephew of Will. Therefore, not being a workmate of Louis is sufficient for being a close friend of Bobby or not being a classmate of Nicholas."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

Economist: If minimum wage levels are low, employers have a greater incentive to hire more workers than to buy productivity-enhancing new technology. As a result, productivity growth, which is necessary for higher average living standards, falls off. Conversely, high minimum wage levels result in higher productivity. Thus, raising our currently low minimum wage levels would improve the country' s overall economic health more than any hiring cutbacks triggered by the raise would harm it.

Which one of the following, if true, most strengthens the economist's argument?

A. Productivity growth in a country usually leads to an eventual increase in job creation.
B. Productivity-enhancing new technology tends to quickly become outdated.
C. The economist's country has seen a slow but steady increase in its unemployment rate over the last decade.
D. The economist's country currently lags behind other countries in the development of new technology.

options: Productivity growth in a country usually leads to an eventual increase in job creation.
Productivity-enhancing new technology tends to quickly become outdated.
The economist's country has seen a slow but steady increase in its unemployment rate over the last decade.
The economist's country currently lags behind other countries in the development of new technology.

question_text:
Although I don't know you, at first glance, you know that you are a military doctor. It is very simple, because you have the demeanor of a doctor, but you are a military man.

Which of the following prerequisites should be added to the above reasoning?

A. To become a military doctor, you must have both the temperament of a doctor and a soldier.
B. Military doctors are more military than ordinary doctors.
C. As long as he is a military doctor, he will definitely show his doctor's grace and military personality.
D. Everyone who has the demeanor of a doctor and the spirit of a soldier must be a military doctor, regardless of his medical skills.

options: To become a military doctor, you must have both the temperament of a doctor and a soldier.
Military doctors are more military than ordinary doctors.
As long as he is a military doctor, he will definitely show his doctor's grace and military personality.
Everyone who has the demeanor of a doctor and the spirit of a soldier must be a military doctor, regardless of his medical skills.

question_text:
"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: First of all, whatever is an ingredient of Mudd Minute Mask is not an ingredient of PENCIL R-MATIC 56. Next, Benzophenone-2 is not an ingredient of PENCIL R-MATIC 56. All this entails that Benzophenone-2 is an ingredient of Mudd Minute Mask."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

Large quantities of lead dust can be released during renovations in houses with walls painted with lead-based paint. Because the dust puts occupants at high risk of lead poisoning, such renovations should be done only in unoccupied houses by contractors who are experienced in removing all traces of lead from houses and who have the equipment to protect themselves from lead dust. Even when warned, however, many people will not pay to have someone else do renovations they believe they could do less expensively themselves. Ttherefore, Homeowners' Journal should run an article giving information to homeowners on how to reduce the risk of lead poisoning associated with do-it-yourself renovation.

Which one of the following, if true, argues most strongly against the passage's recommendation about an article?

A. Most people who undertake do-it-yourself renovation projects do so for the satisfaction of doing the work themselves and so are unlikely to hire a professional to do that sort of work.
B. Most homeowners know whether or not the walls of their houses are painted with lead-based paint, even if the walls were painted by previous owners.
C. Whenever information on do-it-yourself home renovation is published, many people who would otherwise hire professionals decide to perform the renovations themselves, even when there are risks involved.
D. When professionally done home renovations are no more expensive than do-it-yourself renovations, most people choose to have their homes renovated by professionals.

options: Most people who undertake do-it-yourself renovation projects do so for the satisfaction of doing the work themselves and so are unlikely to hire a professional to do that sort of work.
Most homeowners know whether or not the walls of their houses are painted with lead-based paint, even if the walls were painted by previous owners.

Whenever information on do-it-yourself home renovation is published, many people who would otherwise hire professionals decide to perform the renovations themselves, even when there are risks involved.
When professionally done home renovations are no more expensive than do-it-yourself renovations, most people choose to have their homes renovated by professionals.

question_text:
"It is not always easy to see who is related to whom -- and in which ways. The following argument pertains to this question: To begin with, whoever is not a stepbrother of Andrew is a classmate of Harry. Moreover, being a nephew of Harold is necessary for being a classmate of Harry. From this follows: Being a stepbrother of Andrew is sufficient for not being a nephew of Harold."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Consumer research aims at understanding whether users of some products also tend to consume other ones, or not. The following argument seeks to clarify some such relations: First premise: Everyone who is an infrequent user of Giovanni shampoo is an infrequent user of Organix Hair shampoo, too. Second premise: Doria is not an infrequent user of Giovanni shampoo. It follows that Doria is not an infrequent user of Organix Hair shampoo."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

"Here comes a perfectly valid argument: To begin with, it is not the case that Tonya is a loyal buyer of Origins soap. Moreover, whoever is an infrequent user of Lush soap or a loyal buyer of Bio Ionic shampoo is not a loyal buyer of Origins soap. Therefore, Tonya is an infrequent user of Lush soap or a loyal buyer of Bio Ionic shampoo."
 Is the argument, given the explicitly stated premises, deductively valid or invalid?

A. valid
B. invalid

According to China's criminal law, the crime of robbery refers to the use of violence, threat of violence, or other methods to obtain public and private property for the purpose of illegal possession.

According to the above definition, Which of the following acts do not constitute robbery:.

A. Wang took away his backpack worth 10,000 yuan while Zhang was not ready.
B. Hong injected the sleeping pill into a beverage can on the train and let Zhang drink it. He stole 10,000 yuan in cash while he was lethargic.
C. Li entered the house at night and stole 5,000 yuan in Zhang's clothes. Zhang was awakened, and Li fled with his belongings after threatening Zhang with a knife.
D. Zhang owed Liu a 5,000 yuan do n’t pay back, Liu lied to Zhang at home, intimidated Zhang to surrender his bank card and said the password. After Liu went to the bank to take away 10,000 yuan, Zhang was returned.

options: Wang took away his backpack worth 10,000 yuan while Zhang was not ready.
Hong injected the sleeping pill into a beverage can on the train and let Zhang drink it. He stole 10,000 yuan in cash while he was lethargic.
Li entered the house at night and stole 5,000 yuan in Zhang's clothes. Zhang was awakened, and Li fled with his belongings after threatening Zhang with a knife.

Zhang owed Liu a 5,000 yuan do n’t pay back, Liu lied to Zhang at home, intimidated Zhang to surrender his bank card and said the password. After Liu went to the bank to take away 10,000 yuan, Zhang was returned.
